characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: One of the two main protagonists of the show. Mac is a creative, smart, and very warm-hearted 8-year-old. Bloo is his imaginary friend and his complete polar opposite, though they've been as close as can be their entire lives despite their differences. Adorably Precocious Child: Despite the fact that he's only 8, all of the main cast relies on him for advice and planning. He lampshades it frequently. All-Loving Hero: He's friendly and compassionate with just about everyone.The only exceptions are his brother Terrence, Duchess, and Berry. Arch-Enemy: He and Bloo are both the arch-enemies of Terrence, Mac’sBig Brother Bullywho’s determined to make Mac’s life a living hell. Mac is also the arch-enemy of Berry, Bloo’spsychotic admirerwhowants to get rid of Mac so she can have Bloo all to herself. Author Avatar: Unintentionally.Craig McCrackenwas told by his family members that he resembled a younger version of him. While this wasn't what he had originally planned, he ended up rolling with it.

characters.name: Mac
characters.description: One of the two main protagonists of the show. Mac is a creative, smart, and very warm-hearted 8-year-old. Bloo is his imaginary friend and his complete polar opposite, though they've been as close as can be their entire lives despite their differences. Adorably Precocious Child: Despite the fact that he's only 8, all of the main cast relies on him for advice and planning. He lampshades it frequently. All-Loving Hero: He's friendly and compassionate with just about everyone.The only exceptions are his brother Terrence, Duchess, and Berry. Arch-Enemy: He and Bloo are both the arch-enemies of Terrence, Mac’sBig Brother Bullywho’s determined to make Mac’s life a living hell. Mac is also the arch-enemy of Berry, Bloo’spsychotic admirerwhowants to get rid of Mac so she can have Bloo all to herself. Author Avatar: Unintentionally.Craig McCrackenwas told by his family members that he resembled a younger version of him. While this wasn't what he had originally planned, he ended up rolling with it.

characters.name: Bloo
characters.description: One of the two main protagonists of the show. Mac's energetic, hyperactive imaginary friend and complete opposite; without Bloo, Mac would do everything he says and does. Accidental Misnaming: In "Berry Scary", aRunning Gagis that heconstantlygot Berry's name wrong, which was even used asThe Stingerfor the episode. He calls her "Terry", "Cherry", "Jerry", "Mary", "Carrie", "Larry", and "Heather". Acquainted with Emergency Services: In Season 2, "My So Called Wife", he and several other people are thrown in jail for a night after a phony benefactor threw a fake gala in someone's mansion without permission and the owner had everyone arrested for trespassing. Everyone was released in the morning, leading to Bloo saying goodbye to the police officer.Mac:What a crazy night.Bloo:Eh. I’ve had worse.(to a police officer)Good seeing you again, Charles. Say hi to the kids for me.Charles:Will do, Bloo. Aesop Amnesia: Due to his general immaturity, Bloo never seems to learn his lesson from anything. Affectionate Nickname: In the Latin American Spanish dub, he often calls Mac "mi niño".

characters.name: Wilt
characters.description: A lofty, one-armed imaginary friend who loves sports and tries his best to be nice to everyone and gain acceptance from his peers.Is that okay? Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He walks around with just shoes and socks on his feet and a wristband on his right wrist.A flashback to before he lost his left arm likewise has a matching wristband on that wrist.He even mentions this to Eduardoin "Land of the Flea" when the latter feels embarrassed for being naked after Bloo shaves his fur off to make a robe because it has fleas on it. All-Loving Hero: Wilt generally tends to be kind, compassionate, and helpful towards pretty much everyone, with Duchess, Terrence, and Foul Larry being the few people or imaginary friends he dislikes. All of the Other Reindeer: Most of the other imaginary friends take advantage of his kindness remorselessly. Downplayed, as they like him just fine, they just can't resist making him do their work for them. Apologises a Lot: One can say he's a bit too polite. A lot of his dialogue starts with "I'm sorry, but that is not okay!" or at least some variation of "I'm sorry, but....". When he is reunited with his creator, Jordan, he tells Wilt that he needs to quit saying he is sorry.

characters.name: Eduardo
characters.description: A huge, furry imaginary friend who’s timid and extremely sensitive despite his intimidating appearance. However, he's sweet as pie and will go out of his way to protect his loved ones by any means. Berserk Button: Just don't mess with his friends... (or former owner) And while he may act like a crybaby, he doesnotlike being treated like a baby. Beware the Nice Ones: Cowardly and soft though he may be, when pushed, Eduardo makes it very clear that he's one of the strongest characters in the show. Big Ol' Unibrow: He has a giant monobrow over his eyes. Big, Thin, Short Trio: Of the three male imaginary friends, he's the Big to Wilt's Thin and Bloo's Short.

characters.name: Coco
characters.description: An imaginary friend who's a cross between a bird, airplane, and palm tree, with the ability to lay colorful plastic eggs that can hatch into absolutely anything. However, she can only say and write her own name (or variations thereof)and exists in her own universe independent from the other imaginary friends and humans, but she's very amiable and much, much smarter than she lets on. Abnormal Ammo: Of sorts, she lays eggs that can hold anything from bubbles to an anvil, so they could easily be used as weapons if there's something heavy enough in them. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: In "Cuckoo For Coco Cards", she was delighted to find out thatBloo, after being aJerkass to Onetowards her, reveals that hedoeslove her as a friend when he sincerely apologize to her. Bilingual Dialogue: She'll talk in herPokémon Speak, the characters will respond with normal language. Birds of a Feather: Literally with Goo, as they're bothCloudcuckoolanderswith a love for strange things.

characters.name: Frankie Foster
characters.description: The granddaughter of Madame Foster who serves as the caretaker at the mansion and the voice of reason amongst the staff. Alliterative Name:FrankieFoster. Also applies with her real name, Francis. Almighty Janitor: She has to answer to Mr. Herriman and Madame Foster, but she basically runs the entire house all by herself. And as it turns out her paycheck is actuallylargerthan Mr. Herriman's. Anime Hair: Her usual hairstyle is a rather spiky and upwards ponytail, and the few times she wore herLittle Black Dress, her hairstyle then consists of said ponytail being flailed upright. Author Avatar: Inverted. Frankie's character is based onCraig McCracken's wife,Lauren Faust. This explains why Mac, the avatar forMcCrackenhimself, has ithardfor Frankie.

characters.name: Mr. Herriman
characters.description: Madame Foster's imaginary friend; a Victorian, upper-class rabbit obsessed with maintaining order. Animal Facial Hair: The whiskers on his face resemble a thick bushy moustache. Antiquated Linguistics: He talks like a 19th Century English gentleman. Anti-Villain: He's genuinely not a bad person, but because of his extremely strict and sometimes even insensitive nature, Herriman can often comes across as antagonistic. Blind Without 'Em: His monocle. Without it, he can't see anything. Presumably, he's blind in the eye that doesn't have a glass on it.

characters.name: Madame Foster
characters.description: The elderly owner of the titular mansion; very lively for her age. Badass Adorable: She's a cute-as-a-button elderly lady and she knows how to kickass. Beware the Nice Ones: She may be sweet, but you donotwanna trigger her. Big Good: As the owner of Foster's Home, she's the most significant and benevolent character in the series. Blithe Spirit: She and her granddaughter, Frankie, both fill this role.

characters.name: Goo Goo Ga Ga
characters.description: An extremely talkative girl who has imagined LOTS of friends. Aerith and Bob: "Goo" alone is already an unusual name, but her very full namebeing the babbling of an infantis full-on embarrassing. All of the Other Reindeer: Shebelievesshe's a victim of this, sadly telling Mac that nobody likes her. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: She speaks a mile a minute, gets stuck inside her head, not listening to others however much they beg her to, goes on tangents during conversations, and stims to keep herself in check. At her worst, she forgets about important things, such as her repair of Mac's backpack, for months on end. Her issues arediscussedby Mr. Herriman, who believes she's overstimulated by the environment at Foster's. Back for the Finale: After one appearance in "Shlock Star", in season five, she doesn't appear again until the series finale, "Goodbye To Bloo".

characters.name: Terrence
characters.description: Mac's evil 13-year old brother, and one of the recurring antagonists of the series. Admiring the Abomination: His reaction upon seeing an Extremesaur up close for the first time is to stare at it in awe and call it "awesome". There's also the junkyard battles between Extremesaurs and other monstrous imaginary friends he regularly attends. Aloof Big Brother: He and Mac are not very close to say the least and "aloof" is Terrence at his nicest. Arch-Enemy: To his little brother, Mac, who he torments mercilessly. Ax-Crazy: He tries to outright kill Mac and makes very violent threats towards him that shows he can be this.

characters.name: Mac's Mom
characters.description: Mac and Terrence's mother, who made Mac get rid of Bloo in the first place, but is unaware of his daily visits to Foster's. She works all day, and Mac hardly ever gets to see her. Adults Are Useless:Zig-zagged. Mac's mom is usually too busy to see Mac or deal with Terrence's bullying but unlike most cartoon moms, she'snotoblivious to the abuse that Mac goes through, and tries to stop it. But to her, the problem is Bloo, and the series kicks off with her forcing Mac to get rid of Bloo. And the rest of series, she is completely unaware of the fact that Mac visits Bloo at Foster's everyday. The latter is more to do with the fact that Mac's afraid of how she'll react than anything else. The Faceless: Her face is never shown. We see the back of her head and what she looks like below the neck but never her face. Good Parents: She may be busy and absent at most times, and probably doesn't get her younger son that often, but when she's around, you can tell that she's a very loving mother to Mac. Even when she ask him to get rid of Bloo, it's implied she's only doing it just so that Mac wouldn't get bullied by other kids and Terrence. Hartman Hips: She is fairly bottom-heavy, though it's more obvious when she is in her work attire.

characters.name: Kip Snip
characters.description: A greedy and devious Hollywood producer seen in "The Sweet Stench of Success", who tried to exploit Bloo for his own gain. Con Man:The deodorant he sells is actually a phony. It fact, it makes people smellworse. Expy: Of Dick Hardly from theThe Powerpuff Girls (1998)episode "Knock It Off". Sleazy, blonde, greedy, manipulative, has a ponytail, and sells poor products. Thankfully, he's FAR less evil. Greed: He uses Bloo as a mascot for his deodorant to get rich. Hate Sink: He treats Bloo like a slave, starving him and constantly overworking and abusing him, and hasno comedic moments, unlike most of the characters in the show, making it very clear he is supposed to be despised.

characters.name: Jamez Withazee
characters.description: Corrupted Character Copy: OfJim Stark. While Jim was shown to be aTroubled, but CuteNice Guywho happened to have a cool image. Jamez is shown to be aJerk with a Heart of Jerkwho believes that "not caring is cool." Fantastic Racism: He thinks imaginary friends are uncool. Hate Sink: He’s an obnoxious, egotistical bully who mocks people for not being as “cool” as him, justFor the Evulz, he’sracist against imaginary friends, and he has no redeeming qualities. Being aKarma Houdinionly makes him more frustrating. Karma Houdini: He's not shown to be punished for mocking Mac for being a nerd and prompting his followers into doing the same. Monster of the Week: Was the main antagonist of the episode "Bye Bye Nerdy" which was his only appearance.

characters.name: Louise
characters.description: Mac's neighbor and Cheese's creator, who first appears in "Mac Daddy". Braces of Orthodontic Overkill: She wears headgear similar toJimmy, though hers looks pretty dented. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Her last appearance was in "Cheese A Go Go", with the final episode "Goodbye to Bloo" only mentioning heras the actual one moving away, with her new home not allowing imaginary friends, causing Cheese to have to move into the house. Satellite Character: Only really appears when Cheese is in the episode.

characters.name: Extremeasaurs
characters.description: Always Chaotic Evil: They're always violent, destructive monsters that have to be penned up in a massive cage at Foster's. Red (and Pizza)is the one exception, as beingDumb Musclehelped Mac and Bloo befriend him. No, You: Some are more immature than others.Wilt:Sorry, but, teenage boys come up with the grossest imaginary friends. I mean, they're seriously disgusting!Urinal Extremeasaur:I know you are, but what am I?Wilt:(sarcastically)Andrealmature. Sealed Evil in a Can: Because of their destructive nature, the Extremeasaurs at Foster's have to be locked in a large cage. Series Continuity Error: The traits of imaginary friends are implied to be meant to improve the character of those they imagine, so Wilt explaining that they are made by mean teenagers clashes with the friendly ones made by Terrence. Shout-Out: The champion from "Eddie Monster" resembles Pikachu fromPokémon.

characters.name: Scribbles
characters.description: The first imaginary friends created by infants, which are nothing more than a jumbled mass of jiggling black lines (due to the lack of comprehension a toddler might have to envision an actual friend), hence the name. Always Chaotic Evil: Subverted to all heck and back. The home treats all scribbles as nothing but nuisances that only exist because infant children couldn't actually make anything consistent, and forcibly made them aSealed Evil in a Canlike the Extremeasaurs. Turns out they're mindless but capable of helping with chores if properly instructed, no one just bothered to actually learn this since they considered all scribbles useless. Then the shoe ends up on the other foot when everyone relies on them for chores so much that they border onServant Race. Crippling Overspecialization: The scribbles can only do one thing at a time. Give them a list of chores and they will overload and scream until they are calmed down. Even single chores are tricky—for best results, you have to assign each step to an individual scribble. (Bloo demonstrates this with dishes, assigning "you wash, you dry, you put away" to three different scribbles.) Sealed Evil in a Can: Okay, they're not evil, but they're treated as pests at Foster's, so the residents normally kept them in a locked door until Bloo let them out and Mac found out they can do all sorts of things.

characters.name: Cheese
characters.description: Acheese-basedimaginary friend with a childlike disposition; likes chocolate milk and cereal. Absurd Phobia: He is afraid of a lot of unusual things, such as fast go-karts, sheep, cake, and a lack of spoons. Advertised Extra: Frequently appears front-and-center with Bloo on merchandise (especially when the show was running) and in promotional material and ads for Cartoon Network (as seen during the "Yes" era). However, the episodes he appears in can be counted on one hand. Annoying Younger Sibling: He acted like this to Bloo in his debut, annoying him with his idiocy, but he turns outnotto be Bloo's brother when it's revealed that he is Louise's imaginary friend. Despite this, theLeapFrogTag book 'The Golden Paddleball" and the final episode "Goodbye To Bloo" has him continue to refer to Bloo as his brother despite Bloo's protests.Cheese:WE ARE BROTHER LADIES. Cloudcuckoolander: He has a history of unusual behaviors.

characters.name: Duchess
characters.description: An ugly, snobbish, and downright rotten aristocratic imaginary friend. Aristocrats Are Evil: It's not clear if she's actually a "duchess" or if it's just a name. She's definitely evil, though. Art Initiates Life: Possibly, considering she looks like a Picasso painting. This may be intentional, as in the few instances where she shifts her pose she's shown to be completely flat as though she were an actual artpiece. Attention Whore: She gets everybody to bow to her commands because she's able to scream and yell for an indefinite amount of time without even a moment's pause. Beauty Is Relative: Duchess, an imaginary friend who resembles a Picasso painting,considers herself to be beautiful, but thinks Frankie is ugly, despite Frankie attracting several admirersIn-Universewhile Duchess is considered ugly by others.

characters.name: Berry
characters.description: A small, cute, seemingly sweet imaginary friend with a dangerously obsessive crush on Bloo. Abhorrent Admirer: Towards Bloo. In the moments where he doesn’t seemoblivious to Berry’s crush on him, she makes him uncomfortable. Advertised Extra: Despite only appearing in two episodes, she was prominently featured on merchandise in the late 2000s and was one of the few imaginary friends to be available for adoption in an "Adopt a Friend" game on Cartoon Network's website who isn’t a main character. Ax-Crazy:In "Affair Weather Friends", she tries to murder Mac by running him over with a kiddie train, wearing aSlasher Smileas she drives towards him. Batman Gambit:In "Affair Weather Friends", she makes a plan to disguise herself as a rich kid named Barry and manipulate Mac and Bloo. First, she comes to Foster’s, claiming that she wants to adopt a friend, and says she wants Bloo, but Mac refuses to sell him to her, no matter how much money she offers him. She then goes with her backup plan: using an automatic paddleball to lure Bloo away to “her” mansion (which actually belongs to the rich family she kidnapped), knowing he won’t be able to resist it. She then waits for Mac to get jealous and come to the mansion to confront her, luring him into the trap she’s set for him, so she can kill him and have Bloo all to herself.

characters.name: Jackie Khones
characters.description: A small, green, furry imaginary friend with a deep voice, one eye, and a cool personality. Ascended Extra: Started out as a minor background character (who wasn't officially named until the second season) before being given more focus. Clueless Detective:Jackie Khones and the Case of the Overdue Library Crookshows that Jackie fancies himself a detective. He is so so so sosobad at it. Cyclops: His entire head is a single eye. Deadpan Snarker: Has a dry wit.

characters.name: Fluffer Nutter
characters.description: A tiny, pink, squirrel-like imaginary friend who was created by a motorcyclist. Ascended Extra: Not to the extent of Jackie, but she gains more prominence in the later seasons. Badass Adorable: An adorable squirrel, and a badass motorcyclist to boot. Masculine Girl, Feminine Boy: The former to Bloppy Pants, who's aShrinking Violet. Official Couple: With Jackie Khones. Maybe. This is hinted in three episodes, and they were also seen dancing together at theMacy's Thanksgiving Day Paradein 2007 and 2008.

characters.name: Yogi Boo Boo
characters.description: A green, polka-dotted giraffe-like imaginary friend who speaks with an Indian accent and is a vegetarian. Art Evolution: In several episodes in seasons one and two, namely "House of Bloo's" and "Bloo Done It", Yogi is drawn with outlines around his body. But starting with "Beat with a Schtick", he is drawn without them. This is particularly weird, as "Beat with a Schtick" was produced and airedbefore"Bloo Done It". Likely a case ofDepending on the Artist, as the two episodes were animated by different studios.note"Beat with a Schtick" was animated at Cartoon Network Studios, and "Bloo Done It" was animated atBoulder Media. Does Not Like Spam: It's revealed in "Bad Dare Day" that Yogi has a strict moral code on veganism and won't eat meat.Mac uses this to his advantage during the dare-off in said episode, by daring him to kiss Sam Burger (an imaginary friend who resembles an anthropomorphic hamburger), forcing him to drop out. Funny Foreigner: While its unknown whether or not his creator is Indian, Yogi definitely has the accent to prove it. Hidden Depths: He's a pretty good guitarist.

characters.name: Bloppy Pants
characters.description: A very nervous cat friend who is also the lead singer of the band Pizza Party. Ascended Extra: Not to the extent of Jackie Khones or even Fluffer Nutter, but he's given a bigger role in some later episodes such as "Shlock Star" and "Read 'Em and Weep". Irony: He's the leader of his band despite his entire personality. Lethal Chef: In Bad Dare Day, he makes Mexican food for someone being dared to eat it. The person refused out of fear. Masculine Girl, Feminine Boy: The latter to Fluffer Nutter, who a motorcyclistTomboy.

characters.name: Imaginary Man and Nemesis
characters.description: A superhero imaginary friend and his arch-nemesis, a supervillainess imaginary friend. Achilles' Heel: Nemesis' hair and Imaginary Man's masculinity. Arch-Enemy: To each other.Though it's implied that it's all just a game. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other:If anyone but each other attack them, they'll drop their act and become concerned for their safety. Cain and Abel:Subverted. They don't hate each other, they just embody a childish sibling rivalry.

characters.name: World
characters.description: An imaginary friend connected to another dimension connected to a toy chest. He's terribly lonely and abducts Frankie to be his friend. All There in the Script: He's not addressed by name in the special, only in the credits. Anti-Villain: World is the main antagonist of the film, but he's not evil, just suffering from extreme loneliness and abandonment issues. He spends most of the movie treating Frankie quite kindly and genuinely sees her as his friend, and is motivated to trick her other friends because he irrationally thinks that they're going to take her away from him. He only reacts violently when Mr. Herriman gives him a"Reason You Suck" Speechthat hitsallof his worst insecurities, and hisVillainous Breakdownis more akin to a panic attack than a coordinated act of villainy. Once he's given the opportunity to leave the toy box, he sincerely apologizes and is perfectly friendly to everyone. Back for the Finale: Makes a silent cameo at the end of "Goodbye To Bloo". Big Bad: OfDestination Imagination.

characters.name: Bendy
characters.description: Bendy is an imaginary friend who is yellow with black stripes. Seen only in "Everyone Knows It's Bendy", he was brought to the house after his creator's parents couldn't take his troublemaking anymore. Frankie and Mr. Herriman thought that it might just be his creator blaming him, which is apparently very common, but in truth it really was Bendy. Ambiguous Innocence: While he's definitely guilty of everything else he does, there's some debate going on whether or not he was innocent back when he was living with Gregory.Subvertedwhen Bendy breaks a vase in front of the Bloo, Wilt, Eduardo and Coco showing he has always been a diabolical troublemaker and shortly afterwards he fakes his own innocence. Bait the Dog: The beginning of the episode seems to heavily imply that he was sent to Foster's because his creator, Gregory, falsely blamed him for things that were actually done by Gregory himself and didn't realize the consequences until it was too late. Therestof the episode shows that he's just a jerk who causes havoc for the hell of it and is willing to blame others for it. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: In front of Frankie and Mr. Herriman, he puts on the mask of being a misunderstood and sympatheticNice Guywho's always being blamed for other people's misbehavior, but takes great relish in framing others for his bad deeds when they aren't around. Crocodile Tears: Used these to manipulate Frankie and Mr. Herriman.

characters.name: Goofball JohnMcGee
characters.description: What appears to be a teenage boy posing as an imaginary friend to mooch off of Foster's. It turns out that he surprisinglyisan imaginary friend in spite of looking almost completely human, as the only thing identifying him as one is him having a small elephant's trunk for a nose. Ambiguously Human: Parodied. The episode hammers in Frankie's suspicion that heisa human so much that after a while, the twist that he's actually an imaginary friend becomes fairly obvious. Cloudcuckoolander: His speech patterns are more than a little bit odd, even disregarding the fact that he's really an imaginary friend. Deadpan Snarker: He's a laid-back slacker who's prone to making smarmy quips. Easily Forgiven: Despite making Frankie’s life miserable, he does give Frankie gratitude as mentioned below inKarma Houdini.

characters.name: Uncle Pockets
characters.description: The best imaginary friend ever, who was the first one to be put into Foster's. The Ace: Considered the greatest imaginary friend in history and has the longest career of adoption in Foster's. All-Loving Hero: Pockets is kind to everyone, treats them like family and loves all the friends in Foster's. "I Am" Song: In his introductory episode, he introduces himself to the new faces upon his return with a rendition of "I'm a professional friend, imaginary." Big Fun: He is a big guy and very friendly, playful and fun-loving.

characters.name: Red
characters.description: Terrence's imaginary friend, introduced in "Seeing Red". Big Ol' Unibrow: He has a giant monobrow over his eyes. British Teeth: He has the same horribly crooked teeth as his creator. Creative Sterility: He's just a floating square with a face and arms. Little wonder, considering he was thought up by a brainless oaf like Terrence. The Cutie: He's an incredibly kind and gentle friend whose dopey personality only serves to make him all the more endearing and sweet.

characters.name: Eurotrish
characters.description: Break the Cutie: Pretty much her entire role in "Foster's Goes To Europe", where she's constantly denied the chance to go back to Europe and reunite with her family. She finally does get to return home at the end of the episode, but the end credits reveal that her creator can't stand her and sent her to Foster's due to finding her singing annoying, forcing a heartbroken Eurotrish to go back to America. Cruel Twist Ending: "Foster's Goes To Europe" seems to end with Eurotrishgetting her happy ending, only for the end credits to reveal that her creator hates her and doesn't want her back. Funny Foreigner: She’s a hodgepodge of various European stereotypes and it's never made clear which country she originates from (though her owner appears to be Italian). She talks with an Italian accent, has a Dutch hairstyle, wears a Scottish cap, a British flag-themed dress shirt, wooden Dutch clogs, and French music plays when she's on screen. Genki Girl: Very friendly, excitable and prone to bursting out into song, though she spends most of her screentimegetting her hopes crushed. Punny Name: Her name is a play on the word "Eurotrash", slang term used to mock Europeans by Americans.

characters.name: Foul Larry
characters.description: A huge imaginary friend significant for being Wilt's rival in his past. Big Bad: He's the main antagonist in the special episode "Good Wilt Hunting". Evil Counterpart: He's (or at least was) the exact polar opposite of Wilt. Both are imaginary friends who were created to be skilled and talented basketball players, but whileWilt is meek, compassionate, friendly, and a clean player;Larry is obnoxious, aggressive, malicious, and a dirty player. Evil Is Bigger:Invoked. Foul Larry's creator imagined him to be better than Wilt in every way, making him taller andmuchwider than Wilt.Foul Larry:Scared,shorty? Face of a Thug: He's a tough guy played by Kevin Michael Richardson with a relatively intimidating and strong appearance. However, the ending of the episode seems to imply that he has become this, judging by his meek and shy demeanor after hisHeel–Face Turn.

characters.name: Pizza
characters.description: Terrence's first imaginary friend before creating Red. He lived a matter of seconds, only to be eaten. Anthropomorphic Food: An imaginary friend who turned out to be a pizza. The Cutie: Despite having lived in a matter of seconds, he was absolutely adorable, as he only came into the world to love and be loved. Family-Unfriendly Death: He is a perfectly cute and harmless character who is alive for only a few seconds before beingeaten alive,and we get to hear himscream in agonybefore he eventually perishes forever. Kill the Cutie: His death involvesTerrenceeating him alive,right afterhe introduces himself welcoming Terrence.

characters.name: Li'l Lincoln and Moose
characters.description: The primary antagonists of "Emancipation Complication". Li'l Lincoln is a pen-shaped imaginary friend who resembles Abraham Lincoln, while Moose is a hairy ogre. Ambiguously Bi: Li'l Lincoln shared a hot tub with a woman in a bikini pen but also called Mr Herriman his First Lady. Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking: Li'l Lincoln locks Frankie, Mac, and Mr. Herriman in the closet, sells all the imaginary friends to make money so he can turn Foster's into a casino, and makes Mr. Herriman be his maid. Big Guy, Little Guy: Moose is the Big Guy, and Li'l Lincoln is the Little Guy. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Li'l Lincoln is a smooth talker and very good at manipulating people, using his image as the sixteenth President to make it easy for people to trust him. In reality, he is nothing more than a con artist whose main goal in life is to make as much money as possible, and he also plans to turn Foster's into a casino once all the imaginary friends are sold. His true colors are shown when he and Moose have to sleep in Foster's closet.
